reviews_data.review_text: The best desserts in the area. I get something new every time I go and it's always the best thing I've ever gotten there.

My experience at Romeo & Juliet Brazilian Cafe wasn't good this time. The corn cake was very hard and tasted bad; it seemed old or poorly stored. I was quite disappointed, as I was expecting something fresher and tastier. I hope they improve the quality of their products.
Pastel super good! And passion fruit juice! We will be back for more and hopefully Caldo de Cana

Disappointed... Preordered and prepaid for my food, arrived at 7:30 on a weekday when they're listed to be open until 8:00... and the store was closed... Lights out, nobody inside, now I'm kinda just SOL.
I went today and bought 1 coxinha and 1 ham and cheese risole and the coxinha was tasteless and the risole had no ham.
I just love everything about this place. The staff. The food. The location.

Most authentic Brazilian cafe I have had in the US since living in Brazil. Great service and variety of menu items. Went there with my family and we recommend the mousse de maracuja, coxinha, and pastel, the authentic Brazilian hot dog was great as well as the hamburger, but might not be the typical American hot dog/ hamburger experience.
Another overprice store using a "Brazilian Flag" around the block.
It was my birthday and I received a fantastic array cake and pastries. I loved all of it.

OMG — what a great place! Everything felt authentic, and the cake was amazing. We had the strawberry chocolate cake, and it was so rich and delicious. I’ll definitely be back.

I had a hard time deciding what to try, so now my friend is going on Saturday to try the tres leches. Can’t wait to hear how it is!
Little pricey for orange juice and “gourmet cake”
The strawberry cake and pineapple cake were delicious

We stopped in for lunch and have never been here before. I had a hamburger that was not your regular one.

It had corn, cheese, bacon and a homemade burger. It was sooo good.

I then had a health smoothie which had a combination of EVERYTHING.  It was unbelievable wow.

Then to top it all off I had some of the best carrot cake with chocolate frosting on top of it.

If your out and about looking for a place to go get something to eat. I strongly recommend checking these guys out.
This adorable spot serves up yummy treats that stole my heart all the way from Ohio!

My mom and I visited this Brazilian cafe and absolutely loved it!!! They have a variety of Brazilian food that we’ve never seen in the other cafes we’ve went to (we are Brazilian) and on top of that I got to order caldo de Cana, a drink you can’t find in the USA as it’s really rare! Def worth going.

My mom and I visited this Brazilian café and absolutely loved it. They had an incredible variety of Brazilian foods to try—way more than we expected. One of the highlights was caldo de cana, which is something you rarely (if ever) find in the U.S., and it was so fresh and authentic. Everything felt like a real taste of Brazil. If you’re looking to try traditional Brazilian food or experience something different, this place is definitely worth visiting. ( we have visited various other Brazilian cafes in the area)
Great service and delicious savory pastries!  Coffee is also fantastic.  Prices are amazing for what you get.  Sweet pastries are awesome.  Love the banana cake!
Came in for the first time. Cute little shop, customer service was great, the pastry we tried was delicious.

I’m so happy I didn’t listen to the bad reviews & gave this place a shot. I had a great experience with the owners, they are so sweet and the shop is nice and clean. Also the pastels and COXHINAS are amazing!!!! 🙏🏽
Sempre cancela o pedido pelo Uber eats. Não sei porque está lá pra ser escolhido.

reviews_data.review_text: The Pastel was amazing! The brigadeiro and beijinho and dois amores were also very good!
Beautiful, amazing food. The selection is overwhelmingly diverse, curated, and inspiring. The staff are kind, informative, and love to share information about the food.
I like in general, food is good, but we had to wait for cashier.
Good customer service and a wide variety of Brazilian products that you can’t find in Charlotte.

Last weekend I ordered some brigadeiro, beijinho, surpresa de uva, ninho e churros for my baby’s first bday. Unfortunately, my experience with this order of was not positive. The presentation was far below what I expected for a party — many came out of their paper cups, were squashed, uneven in size, and showed a lack of care in the arrangement. On top of that, the taste did not make up for the initial disappointment. Considering the price paid, I expected much more attention to detail and care in the preparation and delivery.
Great sweets, strawberry cakes

We stopped here after dinner to get pastries for dessert. It was our first time, and the server behind the counter was so patient and sweet. She answered all of our questions about the different cakes and goodies in the case. We got 4 items, and they were all yummy. But I was most impressed with how affordable it was. 4 pastries totaled total led to about $10. You can’t beat that! We will definitely be back.

The breakfast sandwiches here are better than the rest....lots of great ingredients and tasty sweets to sample!
The cakes were dry and the food was priceless

Absolutely terrible experience waited more than 30 minutes for an açaí bowl because the “machine went down” when the lights were clearly on and working and the bowl melted within five mins even while in a cold environment. I’m not coming back don’t waste your time and money.
Rosca de coco y Marta Rocha excelente
authentic brazilian breakfast/snacks.

The acai smoothie was delicious and unique per the staff’s recommendation. All of the pastries are fresh, so impressive, and most of all delicious. There are lots of breakfast options on fresh baked Brazilian bread and the cheese bread is to die for. The server convinced me to abandon my iced coffee order to try Brazilian coffee and it too was amazing. I can’t recommend this place enough!
Please put prices on things? There's no reason I need to ask how much every single thing is. Just put the prices up.

Infelizmente não tive uma boa experiencia. Cheguei na cidade hoje e estava com muita vontade de algo brasileiro. Mas esqueceram de entregar um dos itens "coxinha" e a "esfiha" já estava com a massa dura, como se tivesse sido assada de manhã ou no dia anterior.

Unfortunately, I didn't have a good experience. I've arrived in the city today and I really wanted to eat something Brazilian. But they forgot to deliver one of those items, "coxinha" and "esfiha" it was hard as a rock , as if it had been baked in the morning or the day before.

I visited this Brazilian bakery today. The place is beautiful, but as soon as I walked in, there was a really strong chemical smell, kind of like medicine, which was honestly very unpleasant. It felt strange, but I decided to stay since I came to eat there. The smell was overwhelming though, and I honestly don’t know how the staff could stand being inside.
I ended up ordering everything to go. The woman working there recommended a chocolate cake, it was really delicious. She also suggested a sandwich with cheese, I think it was ham, and egg, which was very tasty too. I also got a chicken empanada, but to be honest, it was my least favorite out of everything.

The place is great overall, but they really need to figure out what’s causing that smell, it was hard to ignore.
Our food was forgotten
Awesome!

If you didn't Know, I love a new cafe. This place is a must try. The food is a vibe. From the savory pizza &  hambuger empanada's, to the iced coffee, smoothies and dessert.

I will be back soon

Amo passar o tempo lá!! Além de comida maravilhosa, atendentes extraordinários!! Amo
Local excelente!! Atendimento extraordinário (todos atendem muito bem)! Amo 😍
I like the clean place, cozy decor, super tasty typical Brazilian food. I was well attended by Jair who was polite and professional.

First time in the restaurant and won’t be the last. I drive by this place almost everyday and finally decided to stop in. I got a few desserts - didn’t know what they were cause it’s in Brazilian- but OMGEEEE. They were so delicious. I took some to my work and the kitchen staff went bonkers after trying them.

If you’re in the Matthews area near Crown Point stop in. You won’t be disappointed. The service was equally as excellent as the food. 10/10 🌟

I came here for dessert and ordered a brick sized cake with strawberries in it and it was very moist and shareable. I like the pricing and the kind staff, but I do wish for one more vegetarian option. I got the cheese bread which was okay, and the corn and cheese bread which was good. I will come back here and try ordering from the menu.

reviews_data.review_text: We love the food and the desserts!

I tried this place because my normal coffee shop was closed. I really enjoy the quaint but well lit atmosphere. The only issue that I had was the food. The smoothies were great! I ordered my daughter an açaí bowl. The menu stated that that the bowl came with granola and a couple of other items. You could add additional things like milk powder for an additional charge. I asked for the basic bowl. They had a hard time packaging it to go. It took an additional ten minutes to make it to go and it was spilling everywhere in the bag. I went straight home and I put it in the fridge. When my daughter came home a few hours later she opened it and it smelt absolutely putrid. Like they used juice that was turning already. I threw it out but that was $15 down the drain. I don’t mind paying for quality but that was a complete waste. It seems as if it’s a new(er) shop so maybe they need some time to work out the kinks. I will give them another change. I just probably won’t order an açaí bowl.

reviews_data.review_text:
nice bakery and cafe combination.
simple and small but cozy and comfortable.

it will help to have pictures in the menu next to the names,  to understand what you will be getting, kinda hard to decide what to get, when you don't understand the names and don't know the price of items as well.

of course, if you speak Portuguese or you are from Brazil, it will be not a problem for you to know what you will be getting.

but for others that don't know a lot about Brazil bakery, it will be difficult to pick or dare to try.

still, i will come back, and try more of their bakery.

if you want to have Brazilian food, this is a place that will offer you a variety of items, from sweet and salty, from bake to fried.

We called earlier in the day to confirm the restaurant’s closing time and were told it would be open until 8 PM. After driving half an hour to get there, we arrived before 7 PM, only to be informed upon entering that they had decided to close early due to a lack of customers. So, four customers arrive, and they decide to close due to a lack of customers?!

Comida boa, mas atendimento deixa a desejar, não tem garçonete então só dá pra pedir no caixa, o que gera bastante fila. Comida demora bastante, dá pra ver que falta organização. Decoração bonita pelo menos.

The place has a charming atmosphere, but unfortunately, it falls short in every other aspect. As a Brazilian, I was excited to have a “Padaria” nearby and gave it multiple chances, hoping for improvement. However, each visit left me disappointed. I encountered spoiled milk, coxinhas that were still frozen inside, and pão de queijo that was hard as a rock, and last time (today) we tried a “assado de frango” and as you can see, there is no “frango”. This place has so much potential, but it’s clear the quality control is lacking. Sadly, I can’t recommend it and won’t be returning.

I popped in for a quick dinner. It was my first time here. The server did a good job explaining to me what the different things were on the menu and how they are made. I tried a pastel which is ham cheese bacon and pineapple wrapped in a pastry and deep fried. It was great! I also got a container of the dulce de leche cookies and they were delicious! The atmosphere is cute inside the shop and the staff is so kind.
There is a guy that works there that is extremely rude and he doesn't care if an order is correct or not!!! Shut them down!

"I stopped by after getting my nails done because the place looked unique, and I had never been to a Brazilian café before. Everything looked so good that I couldn’t decide what to get. While I was deciding, a regular customer struck up a conversation with me and recommended a dessert. I can’t remember the name of it for the life of me, but it was a small puff pastry with a chocolate glaze on top. The real surprise was the filling—it was like a cold, rich caramel (similar to dulce de leche), and it was absolutely amazing! I only got one, but I was definitely tempted to go back for more!"
We agreed as friends to meet at this place at 8:30 on a Saturday and online On Maps or Axios states they are open on Saturdays until 10 p.m but when we show up it was close. What a waste
Fantastic staff and atmosphere!!

2 meses atrás eu pedi uma esfiha que estava azeda, os pães de queijo estavam duros e velhos. Tudo bem, pedi pra trocar por um pastel e vida que segue. A torneira do banheiro feminino estava estragada “employees must wash hands” lol what a joke.
Na minha visita seguinte semana passada a torneira continuava estragada e o trocador de nenem estava sujo de 💩. Ok.
Hj foi minha ultima tentativa, pães de queijo do tamanho de um quarter, café frio e adivinha, torneira estragada e trocador sujo.
Triste pq o lugar eh lindo e tinha muito potencial.
Ps: foto do acolchoado imundo das cadeiras.
Que bolo perfeito! Com certeza voltarei.
Peaceful environment,  and good variety of food and drinks!😇😇❤️❤️💯
Bom lugar muitas variedades de produtos só precisam melhorar um pouco o atendimento

pedi um açaí e ele veio todo derretido e sem gosto. não parecia o açai brasileiro, e sim um açaí qualquer americano. não consegui abrir o pote e veio com aproximadamente 1h de atraso. quando eu consegui abrir o pote, todo o conteúdo vazou para fora. não adianta fazer um local todo instagramavel e não ter nada com sabor brasileiro. espero uma resposta.
Great people, food was good. I recommend to go there for breakfast or lunch.
We love brazilian treats and were excited to try this place. We were disappointed. Quality and taste were not good enough and it is too pricey.

Adorable little cafe right beside a Brazilian steakhouse. I used an app called Too Good to Go which consist of getting a surprise bag of food close to the end of the business for a discounted rate. I’ll be honest I had no clue what I received lol, but I will say it was very flavorful and delish. I will definitely go back and try things at regular price for sure.

My husband had to stand by the cash register for awhile and no one offered to help. As far as food, the detox smoothie I ordered was watery and the tapioca shredded chicken was too salty and dry. To be honest, this was our worst restaurant experience in the Charlotte area. I’m Brazilian and I didn’t feel back at home at all. We felt the urge to leave because we didn’t feel comfortable being there. Their food was the only somewhat positive aspect of our experience. Very disappointing for a place that really does have a lot of potential.

Pastel super good! And passion fruit juice! We will be back for more and hopefully Caldo de Cana

Disappointed... Preordered and prepaid for my food, arrived at 7:30 on a weekday when they're listed to be open until 8:00... and the store was closed... Lights out, nobody inside, now I'm kinda just SOL.
I went today and bought 1 coxinha and 1 ham and cheese risole and the coxinha was tasteless and the risole had no ham.
I just love everything about this place. The staff. The food. The location.

Most authentic Brazilian cafe I have had in the US since living in Brazil. Great service and variety of menu items. Went there with my family and we recommend the mousse de maracuja, coxinha, and pastel, the authentic Brazilian hot dog was great as well as the hamburger, but might not be the typical American hot dog/ hamburger experience.
Another overprice store using a "Brazilian Flag" around the block.
It was my birthday and I received a fantastic array cake and pastries. I loved all of it.

OMG — what a great place! Everything felt authentic, and the cake was amazing. We had the strawberry chocolate cake, and it was so rich and delicious. I’ll definitely be back.

I had a hard time deciding what to try, so now my friend is going on Saturday to try the tres leches. Can’t wait to hear how it is!
Little pricey for orange juice and “gourmet cake”
The strawberry cake and pineapple cake were delicious

We stopped in for lunch and have never been here before. I had a hamburger that was not your regular one.

It had corn, cheese, bacon and a homemade burger. It was sooo good.

I then had a health smoothie which had a combination of EVERYTHING.  It was unbelievable wow.

Then to top it all off I had some of the best carrot cake with chocolate frosting on top of it.

If your out and about looking for a place to go get something to eat. I strongly recommend checking these guys out.
This adorable spot serves up yummy treats that stole my heart all the way from Ohio!

My mom and I visited this Brazilian cafe and absolutely loved it!!! They have a variety of Brazilian food that we’ve never seen in the other cafes we’ve went to (we are Brazilian) and on top of that I got to order caldo de Cana, a drink you can’t find in the USA as it’s really rare! Def worth going.

My mom and I visited this Brazilian café and absolutely loved it. They had an incredible variety of Brazilian foods to try—way more than we expected. One of the highlights was caldo de cana, which is something you rarely (if ever) find in the U.S., and it was so fresh and authentic. Everything felt like a real taste of Brazil. If you’re looking to try traditional Brazilian food or experience something different, this place is definitely worth visiting. ( we have visited various other Brazilian cafes in the area)
Great service and delicious savory pastries!  Coffee is also fantastic.  Prices are amazing for what you get.  Sweet pastries are awesome.  Love the banana cake!

reviews_data.review_text: We love the food and the desserts!

I tried this place because my normal coffee shop was closed. I really enjoy the quaint but well lit atmosphere. The only issue that I had was the food. The smoothies were great! I ordered my daughter an açaí bowl. The menu stated that that the bowl came with granola and a couple of other items. You could add additional things like milk powder for an additional charge. I asked for the basic bowl. They had a hard time packaging it to go. It took an additional ten minutes to make it to go and it was spilling everywhere in the bag. I went straight home and I put it in the fridge. When my daughter came home a few hours later she opened it and it smelt absolutely putrid. Like they used juice that was turning already. I threw it out but that was $15 down the drain. I don’t mind paying for quality but that was a complete waste. It seems as if it’s a new(er) shop so maybe they need some time to work out the kinks. I will give them another change. I just probably won’t order an açaí bowl.

nice bakery and cafe combination.
simple and small but cozy and comfortable.

it will help to have pictures in the menu next to the names,  to understand what you will be getting, kinda hard to decide what to get, when you don't understand the names and don't know the price of items as well.

of course, if you speak Portuguese or you are from Brazil, it will be not a problem for you to know what you will be getting.

but for others that don't know a lot about Brazil bakery, it will be difficult to pick or dare to try.

still, i will come back, and try more of their bakery.

if you want to have Brazilian food, this is a place that will offer you a variety of items, from sweet and salty, from bake to fried.

We called earlier in the day to confirm the restaurant’s closing time and were told it would be open until 8 PM. After driving half an hour to get there, we arrived before 7 PM, only to be informed upon entering that they had decided to close early due to a lack of customers. So, four customers arrive, and they decide to close due to a lack of customers?!

Comida boa, mas atendimento deixa a desejar, não tem garçonete então só dá pra pedir no caixa, o que gera bastante fila. Comida demora bastante, dá pra ver que falta organização. Decoração bonita pelo menos.

As a Brazilian, I was excited to have a “Padaria” nearby and gave it multiple chances, hoping for improvement. However, each visit left me disappointed. I encountered spoiled milk, coxinhas that were still frozen inside, and pão de queijo that was hard as a rock, and last time (today) we tried a “assado de frango” and as you can see, there is no “frango”. I tried a pastel which is ham cheese bacon and pineapple wrapped in a pastry and deep fried. It was great! I also got a container of the dulce de leche cookies and they were delicious! The atmosphere is cute inside the shop and the staff is so kind.
